name: CEREBRAL VESSELS
definition: physiologic stenosis ← inertial forces of blood flow diverting main-flow stream from dilated proximal part of ICA with thinner media + thicker adventitia containing many largest branch of ICA arising lateral to optic chiasm major drainage pathway from cerebral veins into internal jugular veins; enclosed by SUPERFICIAL CORTICAL VEINS great variability in drainage territories DEEP CEREBRAL VEINS centripetal drainage of hemispheric white matter and basal ganglia

demographics.Location: lateral to upper border of thyroid cartilage; at level of C3-4 intervertebral disk
demographics.Histo: NO smooth muscle / venous valves → bidirectional flow

dominant_finding: shares waveform characteristics of both internal + external carotid arteries
distribution: lateral to upper border of thyroid cartilage; at level of C3-4 intervertebral disk
discriminating_features: shares waveform characteristics of both internal + external carotid arteries
CHARACTERISTIC bilateral paramedian thalamic infarcts ± midbrain involvement
velocity increases toward the aorta (9 cm/sec for each cm of distance from the carotid
stagnant eddy that rotates at outer vessel margin
traverses internal auditory canal (with CN VIII)

general: shares waveform characteristics of both internal + external carotid arteries
velocity increases toward the aorta (9 cm/sec for each cm of distance from the carotid
stagnant eddy that rotates at outer vessel margin
CHARACTERISTIC bilateral paramedian thalamic infarcts ± midbrain involvement
traverses internal auditory canal (with CN VIII)
traverses hypoglossal canal (with CN XII)
short wide connection between the cavernous portion of ICA + basilar artery
penetrates sella turcica (in 50%)
enlargement of ipsilateral ICA
ectopic vessel crossing the pontine cistern to anastomose with basilar artery
traverses foramen magnum
luminal surface of triangular shape
traversed by septa → maintaining laminar flow + preventing venous reflux into
may replace an aplastic transverse sinus
commonly asymmetric with right dominance
often asymmetric in appearance (see below)
typically not visualized due to small caliber
well-defined 2-9 mm focal filling defects within dural sinus
produce 13-15 mm calvarial impressions lateral to midline
iso- (⅓) / hypoattenuating (⅔) relative to parenchyma
